name: From Arahal to Marchena and Carmona – circular
name_original: Desde Arahal a Marchena y Carmona – circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Arahal offers diverse landscapes with well-paved surfaces and varied elevation gains. The region features a mix of flat sections and climbs, including hills up to 1000 meters, catering to different fitness levels. Cyclists can explore areas with relatively low population density, providing quieter roads for an enhanced experience. The terrain includes agricultural plains, historical sites, and natural viewpoints. Best road cycling routes around Arahal The most popular road cycling route is From Arahal to Marchena and Carmona – circular, a 43.6 miles (70.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route features rolling hills and passes through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cuesta de la Burra – Towards Coripe loop from Arahal, a difficult 67.7 miles (109.0 km) path. This challenging route includes significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views of the surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Plaza de la Corredera – Morón de la Frontera loop from Arahal, a 37.1 miles (59.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain and historical towns, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around Arahal is defined by diverse landscapes, historical landmarks like castles, and green corridors. The network offers options for moderate to difficult rides, accommodating various ability levels. The routes in Arahal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Arahal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Arahal?
A: There are 12 road cycling routes available around Arahal on komoot, catering to various fitness levels. These include 9 moderate routes and 3 more challenging options.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Arahal?
A: The road cycling routes in Arahal range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 9 moderate routes, perfect for those looking for a good workout without extreme challenges, and 3 difficult routes for experienced cyclists seeking significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Arahal?
A: Yes, several routes around Arahal are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995628474/from-arahal-to-marchena-and-carmona-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Arahal to Marchena and Carmona – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option. Another is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuesta de la Burra – Towards Coripe loop from Arahal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along road cycling routes near Arahal?
A: The region around Arahal is rich in history. You can cycle past impressive sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5517287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Morón de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;, an 11th or 12th-century defensive complex. Another notable historical point is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Los Molares&lt;/a&gt;, which can be seen on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6883650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago, Utrera – Castle of Los Molares loop from Arahal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints accessible by road bike in Arahal?
A: Yes, the area offers stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1032251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Acebuche Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural attraction, providing expansive vistas. While primarily part of hiking routes, some cycling paths may offer glimpses or lead to areas with similar scenic beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Arahal?
A: Arahal offers a pleasant environment for road cycling for much of the year. The region's diverse landscapes and well-paved surfaces are enjoyable, though specific weather conditions should always be checked. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, ideal for longer rides.

Q: Are the roads around Arahal generally quiet for cycling?
A: Yes, the area around Arahal is known for its relatively low population density. This often translates to quieter roads, enhancing the cycling experience and allowing riders to enjoy the rural landscapes and historical sites with less traffic.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Arahal?
A: The road cycling routes in Arahal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of flat sections and climbs, and the opportunity to explore historical towns.

Q: Are there challenging long-distance road cycling routes in Arahal?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuesta de la Burra – Towards Coripe loop from Arahal&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 109 km (67.7 miles) path with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, offering panoramic views and a significant workout.

Q: Are there moderate road cycling routes that pass through towns?
A: Yes, many moderate routes connect Arahal with nearby towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Corredera – Morón de la Frontera loop from Arahal&lt;/a&gt; is a 59.7 km (37.1 miles) trail that leads through varied terrain and historical towns, offering a balanced ride.

Q: Are there any green corridors suitable for road cycling near Arahal?
A: Yes, the Green Corridor of the Río Guadaíra is a notable natural feature in the broader region. While specific road cycling routes directly on it may vary, it offers a pleasant, verdant environment that can be incorporated into or accessed from various cycling excursions, providing a more tranquil experience.

Q: Do I need any special permits to cycle in the Arahal region of Andalusia?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for road cycling on public roads in the Arahal region. However, if you plan to cycle through specific natural parks or protected areas, it's always advisable to check local regulations for any potential restrictions or permit requirements.
tips: Marchena was declared a Historic-Artistic Site in 1966, among the monuments that stand out are: the church of San Juan Bautista (one of the most interesting in the province of Seville, Gothic-Mudejar style with Renaissance elements) and the Puerta de Sevilla also called the Arco de la Rosa (one of the access doors that the walled perimeter of Marchena had).

The Cordoba Gate is one of Carmona's most emblematic monuments and a living testament to its ancient history. It formed part of the walled enclosure and the route of the Via Augusta, connecting Carmona with Cordoba. Its function was more propagandistic than defensive, conceived as a triumphal arch to symbolize the power and prosperity of the Roman Empire.

What a beautiful city and what a lovely surprise Carmona is!
This city, which is perched on a hill in the middle of the countryside of the countryside, offers a view on a beautiful agricultural landscape. The Roman necropolis, the walls and the ruins of the fortresses, the Church of Saint Felipe, the Church of Saint Mary, the Seville Gate, the Cordoba Gate and many other civil and religious monuments make this city an exciting place.
